title: Kubic repository out of date
body:
### Issue Description

It's been a few weeks since new releases came out, but the kubic repository still seems to be on Podman 4.3.1 despite many recent package builds. Does it not automatically update for new versions?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Check build status on kubic (or check apt):
https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:unstable/podman

### Describe the results you received

See v4.4.0 or v4.4.1

### Describe the results you expected

Only up to v4.3.1

### podman info output

```yaml
NA
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: What's the recommended way to install yesterday's security release, while this issue is fixed ?

> What's the recommended way to install yesterday's security release, while this issue is fixed ?

If you are using the `kubic` repositories, I don't think there is a recommended way.  The repositories are explicitly marked as unstable.

But we are working with the Ubuntu folks to improve on the Podman packaging for Ubuntu.

i'm working on this now. I'd like to caution everyone again, this repo is best-effort and really shouldn't be used for any production instances. Would be really great to have some additional helping hands for the official ubuntu packaging as @vrothberg mentinoned. Can't wait for the day when nobody will need to use the kubic-unstable repo.

bumped to v4.4.1 just now. Should be available in less than an hour, if not already.

Please reopen if any issues.

Hi,

Thank you for the updates ! 🙏🙏

> If you are using the kubic repositories, I don't think there is a recommended way. The repositories are explicitly marked as unstable.

Very clear, and duly noted.
